insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or POS.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change provides more flexibility and control over the data fetched from the API. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The channelId argument can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or POS. This change does not break any existing functionality, but it provides a new way to fetch data.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data being displayed in their storefronts. This change will allow developers to create more tailored and personalized experiences for merchants and their customers.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over the data fetched from the Storefront API, allowing developers to create more tailored and personalized experiences for merchants and their customers. It also enables developers to handle different sales channels in a more efficient way.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing early access to new inventory APIs.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen package version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: The App Store requirements have been updated to include a unique name for each app, reducing merchant confusion over identical and similar app names.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports the channelId argument, allowing for specific sales channel context to be applied to entire queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: The POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.1 bumps the cli-hydrogen package to version 13.0.1.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen package version 13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and stops those commands with an actionable error, and preserves actionable build errors during hydrogen deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: The @shopify/create-hydrogen package version 5.0.38 adds an explicit app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates GraphQL Admin API, Hydrogen, and CLI with new features and requirements

themes.title: GraphQL Admin API updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate. Additionally, the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId`, and new fields have been added to `ExchangeLineItem`.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released with patch changes, including non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and explicit `~ app` alias in new Hydrogen projects. The CLI has also been updated with new store commands and bumped versions.

themes.title: App Store requirements and POS UI Extensions
themes.detail: A new App Store requirement has been added, requiring apps to have a unique name, and POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 now includes discount allocation details for product bundle components.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use the new GraphQL Admin API features
Review and comply with new App Store requirements
